message:
feat(widgets): add clear_text_row helper + apply it to paginator/stopwatch/timer NoStyling paths

Ninth wave of the NoStyling/Skeleton degradation cleanup.  This
commit introduces a small shared helper for "clear a row of
cells to a known style" so the three remaining widgets that
needed it don't each reinvent the fill loop.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/lib.rs

- New pub(crate) fn clear_text_row(frame, area, style):
    Take a single-row area and fill it with styled spaces
    using buf.fill(Rect::new(area.x, area.y, area.width, 1),
    cell).  No-op if area.width or area.height is 0.
    Handles the full degradation story at the call site by
    letting callers pre-compute the effective style once.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/paginator.rs
crates/ftui-widgets/src/stopwatch.rs
crates/ftui-widgets/src/timer.rs

- All three widgets now:
    1. Snapshot deg = frame.buffer.degradation at the top of
       render().
    2. Early-return on !deg.render_content() to honor Skeleton.
    3. Compute an effective `style` local from apply_styling().
    4. Call clear_text_row(frame, area, style) before drawing
       their per-row content so stale cells from previous
       renders don't leak through.
    5. Draw the actual text (page indicator / elapsed time /
       remaining time / control hints) using the effective
       style.

- Widget-specific notes:
    paginator: the "< 1 / 12 >" indicator and the
      dot-pagination row both route through the new helper;
      colors fall back to default under NoStyling.
    stopwatch: the HH:MM:SS.ff formatted time and the
      start/stop/reset hint row use the effective style; the
      blinking cursor (when the running state flag is true)
      is drawn in a second pass that respects the style
      snapshot.
    timer: same pattern for the countdown + progress row.

crates/doctor_frankentui/src/seed.rs

- Minor follow-on: the seed data generator that produces
  sample widget instances for doctor_frankentui's golden
  suite now also seeds a paginator with multiple styles and
  a stopwatch with an explicit start time so the doctor's
  visual regression suite covers the new NoStyling paths
  without manual intervention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
